Start Your Project
Open for focused work

I build digital products and custom systems for growth, impact, and stability.

I build product sites, web applications, internal tools, and specialist systems where the user experience and the engineering both matter. The work is about more than polish: it is about systems that stay reliable as usage grows, data changes, and teams hand the product off. That means tighter technical ownership, clearer product behaviour, and code that does not drift once it is live.

See my work Start Your Project
How the work starts

Education interfaces that hold attention and stay dependable.

A calm product surface only works when the state underneath it is trustworthy. I start by defining what the user needs to see clearly, what can drift, and what the system has to keep true without asking the team to babysit it.

Clear scope before UI polish.

I map the states, decisions, and handoffs first so the interface is answering a real operational problem.

Working surfaces early.

The useful parts of the product show up quickly, which makes feedback more concrete and reduces late-stage surprises.

Readable behaviour once live.

The end result should stay understandable when content changes, usage grows, or another team has to take ownership.

See how the work runs
Websites and product apps

Public-facing work that has to explain the product clearly and still hold up once the engineering reality gets more complex.

Internal tools and system work

Operational software for teams that need clearer state, fewer manual gaps, and less guesswork around what the system is doing.

Trading systems and fintech

Builds where reconciliation, execution state, contributions, payouts, or trust-sensitive flows need more care than a standard app cycle.

Product reliability and ownership

Hardening the logic, interfaces, and handoff paths so the product stays legible and dependable after launch.

Selected work

Two systems that show how I think.

Both projects deal with trust. One has to keep several moving parts in agreement. The other has to make financial state easy to understand for the people relying on it.

Trading infrastructure

SMC SuperFIB

A cross-runtime trading system that keeps indicator logic, API state, execution clients, and operator visibility aligned.

The hard part was keeping multiple runtimes in agreement without the operator needing to read raw logs to understand what was happening.

Read the SuperFIB case study
Community finance

Stokvel Society

A fintech product for contributions, ledger visibility, and payout flows that members can actually follow.

The hard part was turning a trust-heavy community finance workflow into software that feels calm, clear, and dependable.

Read the Stokvel Society case study
Next step

See the work, then decide if the fit is right.

If the system has to stay understandable and dependable once it is live, the case studies will tell you quickly whether the fit is there.
